name: sendpulse
description: Reads SendPulse address books, campaigns, senders, per-book emails, and the account blacklist, and writes address-book/sender/blacklist lifecycle mutations and campaign create/cancel actions through the SendPulse API.

risk.read: external SendPulse API read of address book, campaign, sender, per-book-email, and blacklist data
risk.write: external SendPulse API mutation, including creating email campaigns that may send to real subscribers
risk.approval: reverse ETL plan approval required before writes

conformance.reason: oauth2_client_credentials auth requires a real token_url; conformance's synthetic non-secret config value ("synthetic-conformance-value") is not a resolvable URL, so the token exchange fails before any declarative request is ever issued, and every auth-resolving dynamic check (including write_request_shape) would otherwise fail identically and uninformatively. Static checks (spec/schema validity, interpolations_resolve, docs/fixtures presence, secret redaction, write_schemas_valid, surface_complete) still run and pass; parity for the read/pagination/schema-projection shape is proven by structural review against legacy internal/connectors/sendpulse (this bundle has no Tier-2 hook, so there is no paritytest package for this wave); the write actions are net-new capability with no legacy behavior to compare against at all (legacy's Write always returned ErrUnsupportedOperation) -- their correctness is asserted by the fixtures/writes/*.json request-shape documentation and by connectorgen validate's static write_schemas_valid/write_path_fields checks.
